stories.title: AUSL Championship Preview — Bandits vs. Talons, Saturday on ABC
stories.angle: The Chicago Bandits survived 11 innings to beat Portland Cascade 6-5 last night and now face the defending champion Utah Talons in the best-of-3 AUSL Championship Series. Game 1 is Saturday July 25 at 1 PM ET on ABC at Davis Diamond — the first professional softball game ever broadcast on U.S. network television. A rematch of the 2025 Championship (when the Talons swept the Bandits). Montana Fouts (7-4, 2.36 ERA) has been resting since July 20. Game 2 is Sunday July 26 at 2 PM ET; Game 3 if needed is Monday July 27 at 7 PM ET. Davis Diamond is sold out for all three potential games.

x_posts.text:

stories.title: Play-in Recap — Bandits 6, Cascade 5 (11 Innings)
stories.angle: Ailana Agbayani singled down the first-base line in the bottom of the 11th to score Taryn Kern and end the longest game in AUSL history. Lexi Kilfoyl entered in relief and was outstanding — 4 strikeouts, 2 hits allowed — keeping Portland off the board through the late innings and extras. Kat Sandercock started for Chicago. Portland's Carley Hoover (9-3, 3.57 ERA) started for the Cascade. Megan Grant's 17-game on-base streak and 10 HRs were not enough to carry Portland through. First play-in game in AUSL history ends in a classic.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Ailana Agbayani. Bottom of the 11th. Walk-off single.

Chicago Bandits 6, Portland Cascade 5. Eleven innings. The longest game in AUSL history.

Lexi Kilfoyl came out of the bullpen and shut Portland down. The Bandits are going to the Championship. #AUSL #softball

article.headline: Chicago Wins 6-5 in 11 Innings. Kilfoyl Saves It. Agbayani Walks It Off.

stories.title: PGF Nationals Championship Day — Tomorrow at Great Park Irvine
stories.angle: Semifinals wrap up today and championship games are Saturday at Deanna Manning Stadium, Bill Barber Park, Irvine, CA. 16U Premier Championship airs on ESPN+ at 8:30 AM PT (11:30 AM ET). 18U Premier Championship on ESPNU at 12:00 PM PT (3:00 PM ET). PGF High School All-American Games also Saturday. September 1 — when D1 coaches can first directly contact Class of 2028 recruits — is 38 days away. Every bracket game today is an audition for September 1.

x_posts.text:
PGF Nationals championships are tomorrow.

16U Premier on ESPN+. 18U Premier on ESPNU.

September 1 — when D1 coaches can contact the Class of 2028 for the first time — is 38 days away.

The biggest stage in club softball. #PGFNationals #softball

stories.title: Lexi Kilfoyl — The Relief Performance That Sent Chicago Home
stories.angle: Lexi Kilfoyl entered the July 23 play-in game as a reliever — not as the starter — and delivered the performance that defined Chicago's Championship run. She allowed only 2 hits and struck out 4 batters through the late innings and extra frames. Kilfoyl keeps her 6-0 record intact. The Bandits' ace switched to a new role in the biggest game of the season and didn't blink. She'll be in the rotation for the Championship Series starting Saturday.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Lexi Kilfoyl went into last night's play-in as a reliever.

She left with a Championship berth.

4 strikeouts. 2 hits allowed. The Bandits' undefeated ace stepped out of the rotation when it mattered most. #AUSL #softball

article.headline: Lexi Kilfoyl Was 6-0 as a Starter. Last Night She Saved the Season as a Reliever.

stories.title: Megan Grant's Season Is Over — What She Built in 2026
stories.angle: Megan Grant's 2026 season is over. The former UCLA All-American finishes with 10 AUSL home runs — second-most in league history behind Maya Brady's new record of 11 — a 17-game on-base streak (longest active streak in the AUSL this season), and a 313-foot debut HR that was the longest blast in league history. She came into this year having set the NCAA single-season HR record at 42. In her first professional season, she was the most compelling hitter in the AUSL. Portland Cascade was an expansion team in its first year. They went to the play-in game. Grant was why.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant's 2026 AUSL season is over.

42 home runs at UCLA. 10 AUSL home runs. 313-foot debut blast. 17-game on-base streak.

In her first professional season, she was the best hitter in the AUSL. #AUSL #softball
